### 1. Understand the Syllabus and Exam Pattern

The first step in preparing for GATE 2026 is to know the syllabus and the exam structure properly. Each branch has its own subjects, but Engineering Mathematics and General Aptitude are common for all. The exam has multiple-choice questions, numerical answer-type questions, and questions that test your analytical ability.

 Read the complete GATE syllabus of your branch and mark the topics.
 Divide the subjects into strong, medium, and weak areas.
 Note that General Aptitude and Mathematics carry good weightage.
 Use a copy of the syllabus while studying so you do not miss anything.

### 2. Make a Proper Study Plan [...] |  |  |
 --- |
| Section | Important Topics |
| General Aptitude | Quantitative Aptitude, Data Interpretation, Logical Reasoning, English Grammar |
| Engineering Maths | Linear Algebra, Calculus, Differential Equations, Probability, Statistics |
| Core Subjects | Branch-specific areas (e.g., Thermodynamics for ME, Power Systems for EE, DBMS for CSE) |
| Numerical Ability | Numerical Answer Type Questions, Approximation, Unit Conversions |
| Problem Solving | Previous Year Question Trends, Short Tricks, Time Management Practice |

content:
#### Profit and Loss Question 9

When the article sold at the rate of Rs.3753 earned a proﬁt of 11% more than that of the loss incurred when the same article sold at the rate of Rs.1432. Find the Cost price of the article.

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

#### Profit and Loss Question 9 Detailed Solution

Given:

SP of the article = Rs.3753

When SP = Rs. 1432, earned a profit = 11% more than the loss

Formula Used:

Profit or Loss = SP ∼ CP

Calculation:

Let the cost price of the article be x Rs.

When sold at Rs.3753, profit = 3753 - x

When sold at Rs.1432, loss = x - 1432

According to the question

⇒ 3753 - x = (x - 1432) + 11% of (x - 1432)

⇒ 3753 - x = (x - 1432) + 0.11(x - 1432)

⇒ 3753 - x = 1.11(x - 1432)

⇒ 3753 - x = 1.11x - 1589.52

⇒ 3753 + 1589.52 = 1.11x + x [...] Testbook Logo

# Profit and Loss MCQ Quiz - Objective Question with Answer for Profit and Loss - Download Free PDF

Last updated on Mar 26, 2026

## Latest Profit and Loss MCQ Objective Questions

#### Profit and Loss Question 1:

A merchant marks the price of his article 60 percent above the cost price. He offers some discount on it and earns a 30 percent profit. What is the percentage of the discount?

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

#### Profit and Loss Question 1 Detailed Solution

Given:

Markup = 60%

Profit = 30%

Formula used:

Discount % = \(\frac{MP - SP}{MP} \times 100\)

Where, MP = Marked Price, SP = Selling Price

Calculation:

Let Cost Price (CP) = 100

⇒ Marked Price (MP) = 100 + 60% of 100 = 160

⇒ Selling Price (SP) = 100 + 30% of 100 = 130 [...] Profit = 25 Percent

Discount = 15 Percent

Formula:

MP/CP = (100 + Profit %)/(100 – Discount %)

MP = Printed Price

CP = Cost Price

Calculation:

We know that –

MP/CP = (100 + Profit %)/(100 – Discount %)   ………. (1)

Put all given values in equation (1) then we gets

MP/CP = (100 + 25)/(100 – 15)

⇒ 125/85

⇒ 25/17

title: SSC CGL Average Questions Solved Problems with Detailed ...
content:
Previous Year Papers [...] #### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 7:

The average of 41 numbers is 62. The average of the first 21 numbers is 56 and that of the last 21 numbers is 70. If the 21st number is removed, then what will be the average of the remaining numbers?

1. 64.25
2. 66.75
3. 68.85
4. 60.95

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 60.95

#### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

Given:

The average of the 41 number = 62

Formula used:

Average = (The sum of all the items)/(The total number of items)

Calculation:

Let us assume the average of the remaining numbers be X

⇒ The total sum of 41 numbers = 41 × 62 = 2542

⇒ The total sum of the first 21 numbers = 21 × 56 = 1176

⇒ The total sum of last 21 numbers = 21 × 70 = 1470 [...] #### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 5:

The average of 23 numbers is 51. The average of first 12 numbers is 49 and the average of last 12 numbers is 54. If the twelfth number is removed, then the average of the remaining numbers (correct to two decimal places) is:

1. 50.45
2. 53.25
3. 51.75
4. 52.65

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 1 : 50.45

#### SSC CGL Average Questions Question 5 Detailed Solution

Given:

The average of 23 numbers = 51

The average of first 12 numbers = 49

The average of last 12 numbers = 54

Formula used:

Average = sum of observations/Number of observations

Calculation:

The sum of 23 numbers = (23 × 51) = 1173

The sum of first 12 numbers = (12 × 49) = 588

The sum of last 12 numbers = (12 × 54) = 648

Now,

title: SSC Profit and Loss Questions Solved Problems with Detailed ...
content:
Previous Year Papers [...] Test Series [...] #### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7:

A shopkeeper increases the price of an object by 40% and then sells it at a 25% discount on the marked price. If the selling price of such an object be Rs. 2100, its cost price for the shopkeeper was?

1. 3000
2. 1500
3. 1750
4. 2000

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 2000

#### SSC Profit and Loss Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

25% = 1/4 and 40% = 2/5

SP of the article = Rs. 2100

MP of the book = 2100 × (4/3)

∴ CP of the book = 2100 × (4/3) × (5/7) = Rs. 2000

content:
Previous Year Papers [...] #### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 6:

A, B and C invested ₹40,000, ₹48,000 and ₹80,000, respectively, for a business at the start of a year. After six months, for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4,000, B added ₹4,000 while C withdrew ₹4,000 every month. If the total profit is ₹6,72,000, then what is C's share (in ₹)?

1. 1,96,750
2. 1,80,480
3. 2,11,200
4. 2,80,320

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 2,80,320

#### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 6 Detailed Solution

Given:

A, B, and C invested ₹40000, ₹48000, and ₹80000, respectively

The total profit = ₹ 672000

Calculation:

After six months, for the remaining time of the year, A added ₹4000, B added ₹4000 and C withdrew ₹4000 every month. [...] #### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 7:

A sold a mobile phone to B at a gain of 25% and B sold it to C at a loss of 10%. If C paid Rs. 5,625 for it, how much did A pay (in Rs.) for the phone?

1. 4,500
2. 4,800
3. 5,100
4. 5,000

#### Answer (Detailed Solution Below)

Option 4 : 5,000

#### SSC CGL Profit and Loss Questions Question 7 Detailed Solution

Given:

A sold a mobile phone to B at a gain of 25%

B sold the mobile to C at a loss of 10%

Total money paid by C = Rs. 5625

Calculation:

Let, the A paid for the mobile phone = x

So, x × (125/100) × (90/100) = 5625

⇒ x = 5625 × (100/125) × (100/90)

⇒ x = 5000

∴ A paid Rs. 5000 (in Rs.) for the phone

Q8. X sells an article to Y at 12½% loss. Y sells it to Z at 9% profit. If Z pays ₹21,582 for it, then at what price (in ₹) was the article purchased by X?  
(A) ₹22,500  
(B) ₹23,275  
(C) ₹21,000  
(D) ₹19,800

Q9. If the cost price of 25 articles is equal to the selling price of 35 articles, find the profit/loss percentage.  
(A) Loss = 28.57%  
(B) Profit = 28.57%  
(C) Profit = 18.93%  
(D) Loss = 18.93%

Q10. The profit earned by selling an article for ₹832 is equal to the loss incurred when the article is sold for ₹448. What will be the selling price of the article if it is sold at a 10% loss?  
(A) ₹576  
(B) ₹540  
(C) ₹625  
(D) ₹640 [...] Q14. A dishonest shopkeeper sells millet at ₹20 per kg which he has bought at ₹16 per kg and he is giving 800 gm instead of 1000 gm. Find his actual profit percentage.  
(A) 52.12%  
(B) 58.36%  
(C) 54.25%  
(D) 56.25%

Q15. A person purchased 40 items at some price. He sold some items at a profit of 30% by selling them at a price equal to the cost price of 26 items. The remaining items are sold at 18% profit. The total profit percentage is:  
(A) 27%  
(B) 28%  
(C) 24%  
(D) 25%

Q16. A person bought two goods for ₹19,500. He sold one at a loss of 20% and the other at a profit of 15%. If the selling price of each good is the same, find the cost price of the goods sold at profit.  
(A) ₹11,475  
(B) ₹9,750  
(C) ₹11,500  
(D) ₹8,000
